page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the arm64-based Ampere Altra processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 vCPU-to-physical-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps under the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The multi-core stress-ng CPU test and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 25% tier, demonstrating the efficiency of its 64 dedicated Arm cores under parallel workloads. Memory read bandwidth is also highly competitive, reaching 4547.79 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 175.98 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the average tier, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6r.16xlarge offers high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance per unit of resource, typical of Arm-based architecture. It is well-suited for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data processing, containerized microservices, high-throughput web serving, and Redis-based caching, where it achieves a strong 84.36 million operations per second.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.16xlarge is an arm64-based compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Altra physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is configured with 128 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside high memory read bandwidth. Conversely, single-core performance remains average to weak. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, containerized microservices, static web serving, and high-throughput caching databases like Redis.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.16xlarge is an arm64 server with 64 dedicated Ampere Altra cores and 128 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance and 16 Gbps bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 128 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for highly parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Ampere Altra arm64 physical cores at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading.
128 GB of system memory providing a balanced 2.0 GB per core.
Strong multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression benchmark performance.
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor virtualization.
Optimized for parallel workloads, containerized microservices, and Redis caching.
